place: Port Jefferson New York, New York

facts.text: Port Jefferson’s first permanent home was built in 1682 by Irish shoemaker John Roe, whose house still stands in downtown.
The village was originally called Drowned Meadow because its waterfront area flooded twice a day, a name it kept until the 18th century.
A local ferry company began operations in 1883, linking Port Jefferson to neighboring Long Island communities across the harbor.
The non‑profit Theatre Three opened its doors in 1969, becoming a long‑standing cultural hub for the village’s performing arts.
The Historical Society of Greater Port Jefferson was founded in 1967 to preserve and share the village’s rich heritage.
Port Jefferson’s main street follows New York State Route 25A, a scenic corridor that has guided travelers along the North Shore since the 19th century.
